commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0fbda54426234ab591e0f07f7d8b52263817f857)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared provider helper that serializes non-image structured tool-result blocks as text across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Mistral conversion paths, with regression tests and a changelog edit.

PR surface: Source +67, Tests +398, Docs +1. Total +466 across 16 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the narrower serializer bug by source inspection: current provider converters only keep text blocks, so structured-only non-image blocks are dropped or fall back. No high-confidence live reproduction was established for the broader truncation and provider-failover session corruption described by the linked issue.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor-supplied real behavior proof and maintainer judgment on the serializer redaction/overlap are needed; automation cannot supply proof from the contributor's setup.

target.title: fix(llm): preserve structured tool result text across providers
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #96857 by preserving structured non-image tool result content as text during provider context conversion instead of dropping it and falling back to `(see attached image)` / empty-output placeholders.

## What Problem This Solves

Tool results can contain structured content blocks from MCP-style resources, JSON/status payloads, or provider/runtime adapters. Several provider serializers only kept `type: "text"` blocks when converting tool results for model context. If a tool result had useful structured blocks but no plain text block, the agent-visible context could degrade to placeholders such as `(see attached image)` or provider-specific empty output fallbacks.

This makes affected agents blind to ordinary structured tool output after provider context conversion, especially when replaying context across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, or Mistral lanes.

## Changes

- Adds a shared `extractToolResultText()` provider helper that:
  - preserves normal text blocks;
  - ignores image bytes for text extraction;
  - serializes other structured non-image blocks as JSON text;
  - redacts inline data URIs before serialization;
  - keeps provider conversion lossless and leaves budgeting/truncation to existing context guards.
- Applies the helper to provider context conversion paths for:
  - OpenAI Chat Completions;
  - OpenAI Responses / Responses transport replay;
  - Anthropic Messages / Anthropic transport replay;
  - Google Generative AI / Vertex conversion;
  - Mistral Conversations.
- Keeps image-capable payloads multimodal while adding structured/text context alongside images instead of replacing the text channel with an image placeholder.
- Adds focused regression coverage for structured-only and mixed structured+image tool results.
- Updates the changelog.

## Evidence

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` passed.
- Added focused regression tests covering structured tool result preservation across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Mistral conversion paths:
  - `src/llm/providers/tool-result-text.test.ts`
  - `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
  - `src/llm/providers/openai-responses-shared.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
  - `src/llm/providers/google-shared.convert.test.ts`
  - `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` / `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts`
  - `src/llm/providers/mistral.test.ts`

Not run locally: targeted Vitest suites were not executed in this checkout because dependencies are not installed (`node_modules` is absent). PR CI should provide the executable test proof.

## Risk

Low/medium. This only changes provider serialization of existing tool-result content. Structured non-image blocks now remain visible as JSON text; image bytes are still handled through existing image paths and inline data URIs inside structured strings are redacted before JSON serialization.
target.author: snowzlmbot

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
